# SR 11-7 — Guidance on Model Risk Management (2011, superseded)

> SYNTHETIC STAND-IN for local development and tests. Historical predecessor to
> SR 26-2; cite explicitly as superseded.

## I. Overview

This guidance describes the elements of a sound model risk management framework:
robust model development, effective validation, and sound governance, policies,
and controls. It was superseded by SR 26-2 in 2026.

## II. Ongoing monitoring

Ongoing monitoring confirms that a model is appropriately implemented and
continues to perform as intended. Monitoring should include process
verification and benchmarking, and should escalate exceptions. SR 26-2 retains
this principle and adds explicit daily-frequency expectations for high-
materiality fraud models.

## III. Validation

Validation should include evaluation of conceptual soundness, ongoing
monitoring, and outcomes analysis. Independent review is a core control.
